title: Aperture Dot Loader
prompt:
Create a production-ready Aperture Dot Loader using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original loader represents a deterministic three-step review pass. Three cyan dots sit inside a pale circular aperture; activating Advance review moves a solid aperture window from one dot to the next and updates an explicit textual checkpoint. The finished state must remain visible and useful rather than looping forever. Use a light Bento composition with #F4F5F6 stage, white panel, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F supporting copy. Cyan is the only bright accent, and the loader card must not use gradients.

Use a native button with a 44px target and an aria-live polite status region. Click, Enter, and Space advance the checkpoint; after the third checkpoint the button resets with a clear label. Move the aperture with a spring at stiffness 330 and damping 25, and fade each checkpoint label in over 140ms ease-out. Use local deterministic state only, with no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network requests, or auto-advancing animation. Favor transform and opacity rather than layout-heavy properties.

At 320px, preserve dot spacing and readable controls; in a detail preview, allow extra breathing room without fixed positioning. Include a visible 3px focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and text that explains progress independently of cyan. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the aperture and using only a brief opacity fade. Return complete client code, CSS-only approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:
*/ .aperture-loader{display:flex;gap:18px;align-items:center;max-width:340px;padding:20px;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.aperture-loader i{width:24px;height:24px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:50%;background:#f4f5f6}.aperture-loader i:first-child{background:#53e8d4}.aperture-loader button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#111;color:#fff;font-weight:800}.aperture-loader button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.aperture-loader *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
